item.title: The wildest allegations in Apple’s trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Apple’s trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI contains allegations that range from employees joking about unauthorized access to Apple’s systems to claims that job candidates were asked to bring Apple hardware to interviews. Here are the complaint’s most eye-catching claims.

item.title: Sam Altman’s space data center trash talk is what most experts already believe
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Responding to Musk accusing him of being a scammer, Altman said, "homeboy you're the one sellling [sic] public market investors on short-term space datacenters."

item.title: Should AI help you get away with killing your spouse?
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: What does a world of total user-aligned AI actually look like?

item.title: Anthropic starts localizing Claude pricing for India, its biggest market after the US
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: Claude users in India are starting to see Indian rupee-denominated subscription plans.

item.title: Waze adds new AI-powered features and customization updates
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Some of the new features are powered by Google's Gemini AI assistant, which reflects the tech giant's broader push to integrate Gemini across its products while also better positioning Waze to compete with rival services such as Apple Maps.

item.title: OpenAI bets on families as ChatGPT goes deeper into households
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: ChatGPT is hiring a dedicated product manager to build experiences for families, caregivers, and older adults, according to a job posting.

item.title: Meta removes controversial AI feature on Instagram after backlash
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: "Our intent was to provide a useful creative tool and to give people control over whether their public content could be referenced in this way," the company said in a blog post. "We've heard the feedback that this feature missed the mark, so it's no longer available."

item.title: Apple sues OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Apple alleges the misconduct was directed by OpenAI's senior leadership, including a longtime former employee.

item.title: Open source AI matters more than ever, according to Hugging Face’s Clem Delangue
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan, Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: Open source AI is booming, according to Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue. The company has grown into something like a GitHub for AI in recent years, where AI builders can share and download open models and datasets, now used by roughly half the Fortune 500. Delangue has seen the same story play out again and again: companies start […]

item.title: SK Hynix raises $26.5B in the biggest foreign IPO in US history, is urged to build new US fabs
item.dc:creator: Kate Park
item.description: The AI chip boom just produced its biggest Wall Street moment yet. Now SK Hynix and Samsung are being asked to build U.S. factories.

item.title: Hugging Face’s CEO on why companies are done renting their AI
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: Open source AI is booming, according to Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue. The company has grown into something like a GitHub for AI in recent years, where AI builders can share and download open models and datasets, now used by roughly half the Fortune 500. Delangue has seen the same story play out again and again: companies start […]

item.title: OpenAI says GPT 5.6 is the ‘preferred model’ for Microsoft Copilot 365 amid breakup chatter
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: OpenAI's new family of models will continue to power Microsoft's suite of workplace and productivity apps.

item.title: Fidji Simo steps down from OpenAI’s No. 2 role
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: OpenAI's No. 2 executive, Fidji Simo, is stepping down from her full-time role after her medical leave proved longer than expected — a leadership vacuum that comes at a tricky time as the company eyes a possible IPO and races to catch Anthropic in the enterprise market.

item.title: OpenAI launches its new family of models with GPT-5.6
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: OpenAI's latest family of models promises improvements across a range of areas, including cybersecurity.

item.title: An AI agent startup just let its agent run its $100M fundraise
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: Lyzr, a startup that builds AI agents for enterprises, used its own AI agent to raise a $100 million round — proof, evidently, that the product actually works.

item.title: OpenAI is shutting down Atlas, but its AI browser ambitions are still growing
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: OpenAI is sunsetting its AI-powered browser after less than a year. But it's moving some agentic browsing features to its desktop app and a Chrome extension.

item.title: Elon Musk praises Mythos/Fable, promises not to ‘cut off’ Anthropic
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Should Anthropic trust Elon Musk to host its models? With about $40 billion in revenue at stake, Musk insists that the company can.

item.title: Can AI answer the $3 trillion question?
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: The AI ROI debate has returned and the numbers are even bigger, as are, perhaps, the consequences.

item.title: Meta enters the crowded AI coding battle with Muse Spark 1.1
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Meta's pitch to users is Spark's ability to handle large agentic workloads, fix bugs, and help with large code migrations — the kind of automation that enterprises are increasingly turning to AI companies to provide.

item.title: New York Times says OpenAI hid evidence in ChatGPT copyright trial
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: News publishers say OpenAI hid tools and datasets that could identify copyrighted journalism in ChatGPT outputs, escalating their lawsuit with a new motion for sanctions.
